title: Why a consistent error envelope?
prompt_md: What is the main benefit of returning a **consistent error envelope** (the same response shape, e.g. `{"ok": false, "error": "..."}`) across every endpoint?
options: It makes responses smaller, reducing bandwidth costs
Consumers (like your frontend) can write one response handler instead of guessing the shape per endpoint, making error handling predictable and reusable
It automatically translates errors into the user's language
It guarantees no endpoint will ever return a 500 error
explanation_md: A consistent envelope means the frontend checks one field (e.g. `ok`) and branches once, instead of writing bespoke parsing for every endpoint. Predictability scales — it's the same reason a design system beats ad-hoc buttons. It does not shrink payloads, translate text, or prevent 500s; those are unrelated concerns.
